title: Eggs, Charred Broccoli & Garlicky Quinoa
summary: Jammy eggs, chilli oil and charred tenderstem broccoli come together in a deeply savoury, satisfying bowl that works perfectly as a desk lunch. The flavours are rich and comforting without feeling heavy, plus it's great for making ahead to take to work.

method.description: Fill a kettle and boil it. Pour the boiling water into a saucepan and bring it back to the boil. Gently lower in the eggs, turn the heat down to a simmer and cook for 7 mins, until the yolks are jammy. Transfer to a bowl of ice-cold water to cool completely, then peel.

Meanwhile, quarter the Tenderstem. Place a large frying pan over a high heat. Once hot, chuck in the broccoli and cook for 3-4 mins until vibrant green and starting to char. Tip in the quinoa, sesame oil, garlic purée, spinach and half the sesame seeds. Stir for 2-3 mins, until everything is fragrant and the spinach has wilted.
Take the pan off the heat and stir through the soy sauce and chilli oil. Taste and season with salt, if needed.
Halve the eggs. Share the quinoa between lunchboxes, top with the eggs and finish with the remaining sesame seeds.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
